content: {"perspective": "Public_Health", "question": "What does the population-level observational evidence — including the March 2024 American Heart Association EPI/Lifestyle conference abstract by Zhong/Chen et al. (NHANES 2003-2018 with ~20,078 U.S. adults linked to the CDC National Death Index through December 2019) and the systematic-review Ramadan literature covering hundreds of millions of Muslim adults — tell us about the long-term cardiovascular mortality and cardiometabolic safety of intermittent fasting, and how should those free-living observational signals be weighed against the predominantly Western, short-duration, supervised-RCT evidence base that shows benefit?", "search_queries": ["AHA 2024 time-restricted eating 91% cardiovascular mortality NHANES 2003-2018 Zhong Chen abstract", "Ramadan intermittent fasting systematic review cardiovascular risk mortality Muslim population", "intermittent fasting population epidemiology long term all-cause mortality cohort study", "time-restricted eating observational study self-reported dietary recall reverse causation bias", "NHANES 2003-2018 linked mortality eating window cardiovascular 2024 abstract replication"]}
